work_unit: H-003 direction-shift: add bearer-token auth middleware to api.py
situation: audit-2026-07-02-008 finding H-003: POST /settings/llm accepts api_key+base_url unauthenticated — SSRF/credential theft vector. All API endpoints had zero auth; CORS was wildcard (H-002 fixed separately). Direction-shift because this introduces the first auth layer to personal-assistant.
action: Added _AuthMiddleware (starlette BaseHTTPMiddleware) to api.py in opt-audit-1782987109 worktree (stacked on H-002 CORS fix). Middleware checks Bearer token via PA_API_TOKEN env var; falls back to localhost-only when unset so unconfigured deployments aren't wide open. Public paths (/health, /docs, /openapi.json, static mounts /web/ /android/) bypass auth. Also tightened CORS allow_headers from wildcard to Authorization+Content-Type.
outcome_evidence: git commit 74fa547 on auto/opt-audit-1782987109, pushed to origin. 1 file changed, 36 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-). Auth middleware added with PA_API_TOKEN bearer check + localhost fallback. CORS allow_headers tightened.
next_suggestion: Next priority pending findings from audit-2026-07-02-008: H-004 (path traversal in POST /inbox/upload, route-fix), M-001 (verify.py _item_counter race, route-fix), M-003 (DELETE /chat-log no auth, route-fix — partially mitigated by H-003 auth middleware now). After all pending fixes merged/rejected, trigger next full audit cycle.

audit_findings.finding: POST /settings/llm accepts api_key+base_url unauthenticated — SSRF/credential theft vector
audit_findings.remediation: Add bearer-token auth middleware (_AuthMiddleware) with PA_API_TOKEN env; fallback to localhost-only
